rules.name: no-reinitialize-taskmaster
rules.evidence: DO NOT RE-INITIALIZE. That will not do anything beyond re-adding the same Taskmaster core files.

rules.name: no-manual-edit-tasks-json
rules.evidence: Never manually edit `tasks.json` - use commands instead

rules.name: no-manual-edit-taskmaster-config
rules.evidence: Never manually edit `.taskmaster/config.json` - use `task-master models` to modify

rules.name: prd-file-creation-conventions
rules.evidence: PRD files should be store at docs/project-name.prd.txt ... Always use the standardized template format shown below ... Maintain strict separation between <context> and <PRD> sections ... Do not think about timelines whatsoever -- all that matters is scope

rules.name: prd-file-modification-conventions
rules.evidence: Change Management: PRD modifications require formal review and re-approval ... Follow the exact section order and formatting specified

rules.name: no-duplicate-generated-types-edit
rules.evidence: USE GENERATED TYPES: Always use types generated from Molecule schema - NEVER create duplicate type definitions

rules.name: no-duplicate-generated-types-write
rules.evidence: USE GENERATED TYPES: Always use types generated from Molecule schema - NEVER create duplicate type definitions

rules.name: ssri-methods-no-validation-edit
rules.evidence: SSRI Methods ... Do NOT perform validation - only construct transactions ... Located in src/modules.rs files implementing the SSRI trait

rules.name: ssri-methods-no-validation-write
rules.evidence: SSRI Methods ... Do NOT perform validation - only construct transactions ... Located in src/modules.rs files implementing the SSRI trait

rules.name: cell-fetching-file-structure-write
rules.evidence: Cell Fetching Pattern: Create dedicated *-cells.ts files for each cell type: fetchByTypeId(): O(1) lookup ... fetchByTypeHash(): Fallback O(n) search ... extractTypeId(): Helper to get type_id

rules.name: cell-fetching-file-structure-edit
rules.evidence: Cell Fetching Pattern: Create dedicated *-cells.ts files for each cell type: fetchByTypeId(): O(1) lookup ... fetchByTypeHash(): Fallback O(n) search ... extractTypeId(): Helper to get type_id

rules.name: service-layer-file-structure-write
rules.evidence: Service Layer: Create *-service.ts for business logic: Encapsulate SSRI trait calls, Handle transaction building and signing, Manage state updates and error handling

rules.name: service-layer-file-structure-edit
rules.evidence: Service Layer: Create *-service.ts for business logic: Encapsulate SSRI trait calls, Handle transaction building and signing, Manage state updates and error handling

rules.name: provider-context-file-structure-write
rules.evidence: Provider Pattern: Use React Context providers for global state: Manage SSRI trait instances, Cache cell data with appropriate invalidation, Provide hooks for component consumption

rules.name: provider-context-file-structure-edit
rules.evidence: Provider Pattern: Use React Context providers for global state: Manage SSRI trait instances, Cache cell data with appropriate invalidation, Provide hooks for component consumption

rules.name: molecule-schema-no-duplicate-edit
rules.evidence: Import shared definitions - DO NOT DUPLICATE ... Use existing types for consistency ... If defining multiple schema, share the base definitions.

rules.name: molecule-schema-no-duplicate-write
rules.evidence: Import shared definitions - DO NOT DUPLICATE ... Use existing types for consistency ... If defining multiple schema, share the base definitions.

rules.name: use-make-build
rules.evidence: Please use make build to build

rules.name: use-shared-error-enum-edit
rules.evidence: Contract Errors: Use the shared `Error` enum from `ckboost_shared::Error`

rules.name: use-shared-error-enum-write
rules.evidence: Contract Errors: Use the shared `Error` enum from `ckboost_shared::Error`

rules.name: no-edit-generated-molecule-code
rules.evidence: Molecule generates code for Molecule Schema. Don't edit the generated code, but use it as a reference. If implementing, do it in other files.

uncompilable: Essential Commands / Key Files / MCP Integration / directory-structure listings: usage catalogs documenting available commands, no obligation attached.
Tool Allowlist Recommendations (.claude/settings.json allowedTools snippet): one-time setup suggestion, not a recurring per-action trigger.
Claude Code Best Practices (context management, iterative implementation loop steps): general workflow advice not tied to one specific tool call.
Git Integration examples (gh pr create / git commit examples referencing task IDs): illustrative examples, no obligation or prohibition language.
Multi-Claude / git worktree parallel workflow description: descriptive pattern, not an obligation.
Troubleshooting steps (API key checks, MCP connection issues): diagnostic documentation, not action-bound rules.
AI-Powered Operations list (which commands make AI calls): informational catalog, no obligation attached.
Claude Code Session Management bullets (/clear frequently, custom slash commands, headless mode): general recommendations, not bound to a specific tool trigger.
Multi-Task Updates / Research Mode bullets: documentation of when to use which existing command, not new obligations.

PRD-First Development Mandate general principles (PRD as foundation, no dev before PRD approval, quality/stakeholder/completion gates, mandatory workflow phases, quality checklist): broad project-management mandates not tied to a specific tool call or file pattern.

CKB dev principle ordering (Visual Prototyping First / Smart Contract Design Second / Test-driven Design Third) and 'use @ckb-ccc/connector-react as global wallet connector': workflow ordering and dependency choice not bound to an identifiable file/command trigger.
ConnectedTypeID / Type Script Args validation requirement ('Always validate and parse args as ConnectedTypeID'): semantic correctness obligation, not detectable via a reliable content regex.
Cell Data Structure Design YAML template ('follow established patterns for common Cell structures'): illustrative template, guidance too soft/broad for a concrete trigger.
build.rs moleculec codegen requirement: one-off project-scaffolding guidance, not a recurring per-action trigger.

Error handling generalities (meaningful dApp error messages, handle signing/on-chain failures, graceful fallbacks for missing cells) and Testing Strategy bullets: general guidance without concrete regex-bindable content.
Architecture hosting/data-storage choices (Netlify + Netlify Functions, on-chain-first storage vs Neon/local storage): infrastructure philosophy, not bound to a specific tool action.
Typical folder structure (contracts/dapp/docs description): documentation, not a rule.
